name: Datadog
description: Cloud monitoring, log management and Cloud SIEM platform. Yields evidence of monitor and alert configuration, log retention and archiving, security detection rules and organisation audit trail.

steps.action: Sign in to Datadog and navigate to Organization Settings > Audit Trail; set the timeframe to cover the collection period and export the filtered events as CSV
steps.permissions_required: Audit Trail Read permission (Datadog Admin Role by default)
steps.security_note: Use a role granting only Audit Trail Read for evidence collection, not full Datadog Admin
steps.audit_note: Audit Trail events are recorded by Datadog with actor, action and timestamp; retention is up to 90 days in-app

steps.action: Navigate to Monitors > Manage Monitors, filter to the in-scope monitors (e.g. by team or service tag) and capture dated screenshots of the list; open key monitors to capture alert conditions and notification recipients
steps.audit_note: Capture both the monitor query and the notification targets — they evidence that alerting reaches the right people

steps.action: Navigate to Logs > Configuration and capture the Indexes page showing per-index retention periods, and the Archives tab showing any configured cloud storage archives
steps.audit_note: Index retention plus archive configuration together evidence the log retention control

steps.action: Navigate to the Cloud SIEM Detection Rules page and capture the list of enabled detection rules, including severity and rule type
Upload the CSV export and screenshots to the evidence repository with evidence ID reference and collection date
steps.security_note: Review captures for API keys, application keys or personal data before uploading

steps.action: Build (or reuse) a compliance evidence dashboard showing monitor status, log ingestion volume and security signal widgets, then select Share > Schedule report on the dashboard
Set the report schedule and timeframe to match the evidence collection cadence (e.g. weekly, covering the previous 7 days) and add the compliance mailbox as recipient
steps.security_note: Limit recipients to authorised personnel; the PDF may reveal infrastructure detail
steps.audit_note: Reports are generated as timestamped PDFs delivered by email, Slack or Microsoft Teams

steps.action: In the Logs Explorer, create Scheduled CSV Reports for any saved views used as evidence (e.g. authentication or change events), delivered by email on the same cadence
steps.audit_note: CSV reports capture the underlying events, complementing the dashboard PDF summary

steps.action: In Organization Settings > Audit Trail Settings, enable Archiving (Store Events) to Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage or Azure Storage so audit events are retained beyond the 90-day in-app limit
steps.audit_note: Archived audit events upload roughly every 15 minutes, giving durable evidence without custom code

steps.action: Verify the first scheduled deliveries arrive and file them in the evidence repository; diarise a periodic check that schedules remain active

Create a dedicated API key under Organization Settings > API Keys and a scoped application key under Organization Settings > Application Keys for a service account, limiting the application key to read permissions (audit_trail_read, monitors_read, security_monitoring_rules_read, dashboards_read)
steps.permissions_required: Datadog Admin Role (or User Access Manage) to create service account keys
steps.security_note: Store both keys in a secrets manager; requests authenticate with the DD-API-KEY and DD-APPLICATION-KEY headers. Rotate keys on a defined schedule.
steps.audit_note: Key creation and use are recorded in the Datadog Audit Trail

steps.action: Schedule a script to call POST /api/v2/audit/events/search with a filter for the collection window, paginating with the returned cursor until complete
steps.audit_note: Use non-overlapping time windows between runs to avoid gaps or duplicates

steps.action: Snapshot alerting configuration daily with GET /api/v1/monitor (all monitor definitions, thresholds and notification targets)
steps.audit_note: Store each JSON snapshot with retrieval timestamp — diffs between snapshots evidence change control over alerting

steps.action: Snapshot security and retention configuration with GET /api/v2/security_monitoring/rules (detection rules) and GET /api/v1/logs/config/indexes (per-index retention)
Run the collection via cron or a CI/CD pipeline, writing timestamped JSON outputs to the evidence repository
steps.security_note: Inject keys from the secrets manager at runtime — never store them in code or plaintext configuration; use your region's API host (e.g. api.datadoghq.com or api.datadoghq.eu)

steps.action: Implement error handling and alerting for failed runs, and validate collected audit event counts against a baseline to flag collection gaps
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should trigger re-collection within SLA; volume validation provides assurance of completeness

L4.title: Datadog Managed Evidence Pipeline with Metrics

steps.action: Configure Audit Trail forwarding to a custom destination (HTTP endpoint, Splunk or Elasticsearch) so organisation audit events stream continuously into your evidence platform or SIEM
steps.security_note: Use an HTTPS destination with authentication headers stored as secrets in the destination configuration

steps.action: On the Log Archiving & Forwarding page, configure log archives to cloud storage and Custom Destinations forwarding for in-scope log streams, giving durable long-term retention plus live delivery
steps.audit_note: Archives provide the immutable long-term record; forwarding provides the continuous feed

steps.action: Create an Audit Trail monitor that alerts on collection-relevant changes (e.g. index retention edits, archive deletion, detection rules disabled) so configuration drift is flagged in near real time
steps.audit_note: Using Datadog to monitor its own audit stream turns evidence collection into a detective control

steps.action: Build completeness metrics in the receiving platform: audit events per day, forwarded log volume per source, delivery latency; alert when any stream drops below its rolling baseline
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are key evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Add automated quality checks on the collected data — schema validation, time-series gap detection and duplicate suppression — before it is filed as evidence
Generate an automated quarterly collection health report covering pipeline uptime, gap incidents and any SLA breaches
